chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Dear students, Welcome to Gate Smashers

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: In today's video I'm going to explain Hadoop ecosystem

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So in Hadoop ecosystem there are many different components

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Some components are mandatory, which are very important

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Without them it won't work

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: But for some extra functionalities we use different components

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: What are the components, how are they, why are they used

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: I'll explain all of them one by one with example

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So basically what we need is that main work of Hadoop is going on

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: But what extra functionalities are needed along with it

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Which has proper monitoring, proper management

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: We need security feature, we need scalability feature

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So for these different features we use different components

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So let's start, first of all we get the mandatory component

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: That is HDFS, Hadoop Distributed File System

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Whose main purpose is to manage and store data in a distributed manner

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So that we can easily access it

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So all this storage work is handled by HDFS

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: This is a file system like in our normal laptop, in our computer

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Like Windows is using, so in Windows there's a file system NTFS

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: If Linux is using then NFS, there are different file systems

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Similarly this is Hadoop's file system, so this is mandatory part

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: By the way I have made a video on it separately

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Which components it works with, so you must check that video

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Then we get second, YARN

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: What is YARN? Yet Another Resource Negotiator

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So what is the purpose of YARN here

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Actually before explaining YARN, let me tell you about MapReduce

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: MapReduce is actually used for data processing

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Because we kept data in distributed way

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So we need a component which can access data parallelly

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: And process it and convert it in output

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So MapReduce is used for that purpose

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: But when Hadoop's first version came 1.0

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So Hadoop's MapReduce feature was

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Which had data processing

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: That how to process data and at the same time how to manage resources

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So all those purposes of data processing and management was of this only

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: But in later versions they did

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: That we brought many things on MapReduce

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So because of that it got slow

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Data kept increasing and it got slow

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So that's why it was removed from here

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: And after removing it, a separate component was made, that is YARN

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So what is the purpose of YARN

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: How to manage resources, how to schedule jobs

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: All that work is now seen by YARN

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: You can understand with a simple example

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So in our laptop, the CPU is MapReduce

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: In our laptop, the operating system like Windows, Linux, we use that is YARN

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: In our laptop, the file system which we use

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Which comes inside Windows, like it comes inside our operating system

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: But if you're using NTFS or NFS

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So that file system is its work

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So you can understand it with this example

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So you know these three purposes, it's a mandatory portion

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: After that comes the story of Flume and Scoop

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Now both of these are used for data collection

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Used for data ingestion

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Like as an example, take the same laptop example

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: You're using a laptop, you're using your computer

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Where does data come from in that laptop computer

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Where does data input come from

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: You're downloading, you're inserting from a pen drive

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: You're inserting from an external hard drive

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Data comes like this only

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So that is called data ingestion, data coming in your system

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So where is the data coming from in your system, in Hadoop system

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: It's coming from these two components

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: One is Flume and the other is Scoop

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Scoop is for structured data

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: And Flume is for unstructured or semi-structured data

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Means you're using an application of MySQL

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: You're using SQL server and you're using ACL

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So it generates structured data

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Now I have to bring that structured data in Hadoop, in HDFS

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So whom I'll use? Scoop

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: I have to take it from HDFS to SQL server

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: In MySQL, whom I'll use? Scoop

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: But now a days we're dealing more with unstructured data

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Photos, videos, live streaming we're doing

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So where live streaming comes, streaming data comes

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: From where continuously data is coming, log files are coming

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So there Flume helps

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So Flume unstructured, data streaming

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Collectively the real time data that is coming

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: For that we use Flume and it is used for structured data

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So this is the main point here

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Then HBase

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: What is HBase? We store data in column base

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: What happens is, generally in relational database DBMS

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: We read that if I have to store data

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Then first I have to make a structure

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: You know we make table, create table, table name

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: We make all those rows and columns

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Then we store the data

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Here there is no such rule

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Here there is no restriction that first make structure then data

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: It says bring data and store it without any structure

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: That's why we use NoSQL concept here

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: And here this HBase is actually column oriented

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: This was row, we used to store data in rows in RDBMS

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Here columns are formed

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: This is taken, Google's big table project

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: This was specially designed by modeling it

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So that large amount of data can be stored

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Then Cloud Era

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Now see here we have all these features

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: That it processes data, manages data, stores data

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: In which way data has to be stored in the form of columns

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Where data is coming from, that is also known

chunks.text: Now here the output that came after processing my data

chunks.text: How to analyze that output?

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: How to see that output?

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Or you can say data analysis

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Or for different purposes like Hive

chunks.text: Why is Hive used?

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: For SQL query

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Normally we do Java coding here

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So in Java coding, we have to write very big codes of mapper, reducer, driver

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: If I have to write a command in the form of SQL

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Means I know SQL and I want to use it in Hadoop

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Then I can use Hive

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So for structured data you can use Hive

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: If your data is structured then only Hive can be used

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Because it is based on SQL commands

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Then what is Pig?

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Pig is also a component that is used for scripting

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: What is the purpose of scripting?

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: The same thing I told you that when we write mapper, reducer, driver class

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Then we have to write very big codes

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So what Pig tells you

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: That the code you are writing in 200 lines, 300 lines

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: Finish it in 10 to 20 lines

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: So somewhere your programming line of code is very big

chunks.title: Hadoop Ecosystem All Components
chunks.text: It is trying to reduce it, that is a Pig

text: Dear students, Welcome to Gate Smashers In today's video I'm going to explain Hadoop ecosystem So in Hadoop ecosystem there are many different components Some components are mandatory, which are very important Without them it won't work But for some extra functionalities we use different components What are the components, how are they, why are they used I'll explain all of them one by one with example So basically what we need is that main work of Hadoop is going on But what extra functionalities are needed along with it Which has proper monitoring, proper management We need security feature, we need scalability feature So for these different features we use different components So let's start, first of all we get the mandatory component That is HDFS, Hadoop Distributed File System Whose main purpose is to manage and store data in a distributed manner So that we can easily access it So all this storage work is handled by HDFS This is a file system like in our normal laptop, in our computer Like Windows is using, so in Windows there's a file system NTFS If Linux is using then NFS, there are different file systems Similarly this is Hadoop's file system, so this is mandatory part By the way I have made a video on it separately Which components it works with, so you must check that video Then we get second, YARN What is YARN? Yet Another Resource Negotiator So what is the purpose of YARN here Actually before explaining YARN, let me tell you about MapReduce MapReduce is actually used for data processing Because we kept data in distributed way So we need a component which can access data parallelly And process it and convert it in output So MapReduce is used for that purpose But when Hadoop's first version came 1.0 So Hadoop's MapReduce feature was Which had data processing That how to process data and at the same time how to manage resources So all those purposes of data processing and management was of this only But in later versions they did That we brought many things on MapReduce So because of that it got slow Data kept increasing and it got slow So that's why it was removed from here And after removing it, a separate component was made, that is YARN So what is the purpose of YARN How to manage resources, how to schedule jobs All that work is now seen by YARN You can understand with a simple example So in our laptop, the CPU is MapReduce In our laptop, the operating system like Windows, Linux, we use that is YARN In our laptop, the file system which we use Which comes inside Windows, like it comes inside our operating system But if you're using NTFS or NFS So that file system is its work So you can understand it with this example So you know these three purposes, it's a mandatory portion After that comes the story of Flume and Scoop Now both of these are used for data collection Used for data ingestion Like as an example, take the same laptop example You're using a laptop, you're
